details.name: ABS (Anti-lock Braking System)
details.desc: The overall Anti-lock Braking System encompassing all its sub-components and their interactions.

details.name: ABS ECU
details.desc: The primary Electronic Control Unit responsible for processing sensor data, implementing ABS logic, and controlling actuators. It includes MCU, firmware, and calibration data.

details.name: Brake Control Logic
details.desc: The core software module within the ABS ECU that implements the ABS algorithms, making decisions based on wheel speed, vehicle dynamics, and driver input.

details.name: Wheel Speed Sensors
details.desc: Sensors that measure the rotational speed of each wheel, providing critical input data to the ABS ECU. Communication typically via CAN.

details.name: Hydraulic Control Unit (HCU)
details.desc: The actuator responsible for modulating brake fluid pressure to individual wheels based on commands from the ABS ECU, enabling the anti-lock function. Communicates via CAN.

details.name: UDS Diagnostic Interface
details.desc: The interface supporting the Unified Diagnostic Services (UDS) protocol, allowing for diagnostics, parameter reading, and potential actuation control through the OBD-II port. This is an external-facing trust boundary.

Derivations.name: Firmware Tampering via UDS
Derivations.damage_scene: An attacker with physical access to the OBD-II port exploits a vulnerability in the UDS diagnostic interface to overwrite or corrupt the ABS ECU firmware, leading to erratic braking behavior or complete system failure.

Derivations.name: CAN Bus Message Injection
Derivations.damage_scene: An attacker injects forged CAN messages (e.g., false wheel speed signals) onto the ABS network, overwhelming the ABS ECU and causing it to shut down or malfunction, disabling ABS functionality.

Derivations.name: User Data Corruption
Derivations.damage_scene: An attacker gains unauthorized access to the ABS ECU and corrupts critical calibration data within the brake control logic, leading to incorrect ABS activation thresholds and compromised braking performance.

Derivations.name: Physical Debug Port Access
Derivations.damage_scene: An attacker gains physical access to a debug port (e.g., JTAG or UART) on the ABS ECU, allowing them to extract sensitive information such as proprietary algorithms, cryptographic keys, or firmware secrets.

Derivations.name: Spoofed Wheel Speed Sensor Data
Derivations.damage_scene: An attacker manipulates or spoofs the signals from wheel speed sensors, sending false data to the ABS ECU. This could cause the ABS to activate unnecessarily or fail to activate when needed, leading to loss of vehicle control.

Details.Name: Firmware Tampering via UDS
Details.Description: An attacker with physical access to the OBD-II port exploits a vulnerability in the UDS diagnostic interface to overwrite or corrupt the ABS ECU firmware, leading to erratic braking behavior or complete system failure.

Details.Name: CAN Bus Message Injection
Details.Description: An attacker injects forged CAN messages (e.g., false wheel speed signals) onto the ABS network, overwhelming the ABS ECU and causing it to shut down or malfunction, disabling ABS functionality.

Details.Name: User Data Corruption
Details.Description: An attacker gains unauthorized access to the ABS ECU and corrupts critical calibration data within the brake control logic, leading to incorrect ABS activation thresholds and compromised braking performance.

Details.Name: Physical Debug Port Access
Details.Description: An attacker gains physical access to a debug port (e.g., JTAG or UART) on the ABS ECU, allowing them to extract sensitive information such as proprietary algorithms, cryptographic keys, or firmware secrets.

Details.Name: Spoofed Wheel Speed Sensor Data
Details.Description: An attacker manipulates or spoofs the signals from wheel speed sensors, sending false data to the ABS ECU. This could cause the ABS to activate unnecessarily or fail to activate when needed, leading to loss of vehicle control.

threat_scenarios.name: Tampering of ABS ECU Firmware
threat_scenarios.description: Tampering occurred due to Loss of Integrity on ABS ECU Firmware.

threat_scenarios.name: Denial of ABS ECU
threat_scenarios.description: Denial occurred due to Loss of Availability on ABS ECU.

threat_scenarios.name: Tampering of ABS Calibration Data
threat_scenarios.description: Tampering occurred due to Loss of Integrity on ABS Calibration Data.
